title: aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla
intro: Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla, commonly known as the Philippine agarwood, is a plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Meliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The therapeutic actions of Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla include its ability to act as an antioxidant and an anti-inflammatory agent, making it useful in the treatment of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include its essential oils, which contain compounds such as limonene, β-caryophyllene, and α-pinene, contributing to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla has many benefits, such as its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, gout, and asthma. Its antioxidant properties also contribute to reducing the risk of chronic diseases, including heart disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Additionally, the plant's antibacterial and antifungal properties make it effective in treating infections and promoting wound healing, improving the overall quality of life and reducing the need for antibiotics. By incorporating Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved health, increased energy, and enhanced mental well-being, leading to a more balanced and fulfilling life.
intro_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla has shown moderate antitumor activity and antibacterial activities.
constituents: Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which possess various medicinal properties. These compounds, including aglaiol, aglavin, and aglacon, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, potentially improving the management of conditions like eczema, acne, and flu. The plant's constituents also contain cardioactive compounds like cardiac glycosides, which can help regulate heart rate and blood pressure, thus contributing to the prevention of cardiovascular diseases. Furthermore, the presence of sesquiterpenes and coumarins in Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla may provide analgesic and anticoagulant effects, making it a promising natural remedy for pain relief and blood clot prevention.
parts: Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and bark, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine. The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots and bark of Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and limonoids, which have been reported to possess antibacterial and antifungal activities. Additionally, the plant's seeds have been found to contain triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties.
preparations: Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla has many herbal preparations, such as tea, tinctures, and infusions. The tea is used for its antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever, while the tincture is utilized as an anti-inflammatory agent to alleviate pain and swelling. The infusion, often made with the leaves and bark of the plant, is applied topically to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. These herbal preparations can also be used as a natural remedy for digestive issues, with the tea and tincture helping to soothe stomach discomfort and promote digestive health.
side_effects: Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its potential to interact with medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant may lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues. Additionally, improper use of Aglaia lawii subsp. submonophylla may also cause dizziness, headaches, and tremors in susceptible individuals. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to minimize the risk of adverse effects.
